config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa dan tirani mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu, melainkan yang menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum dengan hak pribadi secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a blanket rule, because in the United States that idea has historically been used to justify trampling minority rights and personal freedoms. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute when someone is harming others, so public health, safety, and basic order can justify limits. The key point is that the burden should be on society to prove a restriction is necessary and narrowly tailored, not on individuals to surrender their rights by default. A healthy society protects the common good by respecting rights first, not by treating people as expendable for the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia kita juga tahu betapa mudahnya “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak boleh sewenang-wenang. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi hak dasar warganya, termasuk kelompok kecil dan minoritas, bukan yang mengorbankan mereka demi suara mayoritas. Bagi saya, keseimbangan itu penting, tetapi titik awalnya tetap harus dari penghormatan pada hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the statement as a general principle, because in the United States “society first” has too often been the language used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. That said, I do not treat individual rights as absolute in every case, since real harms like disease spread, violence, and fraud justify limited rules that protect others. What matters is that any restriction be necessary, proportionate, and tightly limited, instead of assuming people must yield their rights whenever the majority wants it. In my view, a free society serves the common good best when it starts by protecting the individual, not by subordinating the individual to the crowd.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik, minoritas, atau orang yang lemah posisinya. Kepentingan umum memang bisa membenarkan pembatasan tertentu, misalnya untuk kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas ingin demikian. Kalau hak individu selalu ditempatkan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat, yang terjadi justru rawan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in the United States I have seen how "society first" can become a blank check for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. That does not mean individual rights are unlimited; public health, safety, and fraud laws can justify real restrictions when they are necessary and narrowly tailored. But the default should not be that people must give up their rights whenever the majority says so, because majorities can be wrong and often target unpopular groups first. To me, a legitimate society protects the common good by setting strong limits on power, not by putting the individual below the crowd.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
